entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum L., the crop being studied for disease resistance

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: A major soil-borne disease affecting chickpea production worldwide

entities.name: FUSARIUM OXYSPORUM F. SP. CICERIS
entities.description: The soil-borne fungal pathogen that causes Fusarium wilt in chickpea

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea genotypes to withstand or tolerate Fusarium wilt

entities.name: MULTI-ENVIRONMENT SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating genotypes across multiple locations and years to identify stable and durable resistance

entities.name: ICRISAT PATANCHERU
entities.description: A research facility and testing site in India

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the multi-environment validation of chickpea genotypes was conducted

entities.name: BADNAPUR
entities.description: A specific testing location where most genotypes showed resistance

entities.name: DHOLI
entities.description: A specific testing location where most genotypes were susceptible

entities.name: KANPUR
entities.description: A specific testing location where most genotypes were susceptible

entities.name: GGE BIPLOT ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to analyze genotype and environment interaction for the selection of stable genotypes

entities.name: GENOTYPE X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The variation in trait expression due to the interaction between genetic makeup and environmental conditions

entities.name: HIGHLY RESISTANT GENOTYPES
entities.description: A group of six chickpea genotypes (ICCVs 98505, 07105, 07111, 07305, 08113, 93706) showing high resistance and stability

entities.name: MODERATELY RESISTANT GENOTYPES
entities.description: A group of eight chickpea genotypes showing moderate resistance to Fusarium wilt

entities.name: RESISTANCE BREEDING PROGRAM
entities.description: A program aimed at developing cultivars with durable resistance to Fusarium wilt
